title: aira flexuosa var. impuber
intro: Aira flexuosa var. impuber, also known as wood meadow-grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Aira flexuosa var. impuber include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and act as a diuretic, making it a useful remedy for urinary tract issues. Aira flexuosa var. impuber can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be done under the guidance of a medical professional.
